text:
Amoeba (アメイバ, Ameiba) is one of the Heavens Arena's Floor Masters. He only appears in Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission. Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission 

Appearance

Amoeba is spindly with long limbs. He has a skeletal face, topped by a shock of unkempt, greenish hair. He has a small chin and thick purple lips. The most striking feature on his face is his eyes: his right eye's sclera, iris, and pupil are completely yellow. A visor covers the other. 

In the Heavens Arena, he wears golden bracelets, a matching golden collar, a white and azure striped shirt that leaves his belly bare, a white belt with a round golden buckle, dark blue jeans, possibly with bell bottoms, and brown shoes. The right sleeve of his shirt is longer and baggier to hide a small cannon. On his chest, the stripes form the motif of a vortex. 

Personality

Not much is known about Amoeba's personality due to the limited number of scenes he appears in during Hunter × Hunter: The Last Mission, but he is often seen as eccentric and strange, even by the other Floor Masters, particularly Gama who is weirded out by Amoeba's strange movements during training. 

Amoeba also appears to be impatient, sometimes speaking with a whiny tone, particularly before the Battle Olympia begins when voicing his complaints to Gama. 

Plot

The Last Mission

As a Floor Master, Amoeba takes part in the Battle Olympia at Heavens Arena along with twenty other fighters. He is first seen walking into the arena with the other fighters during Cocco's introduction to the event. 

Before the tournament's first match, Amoeba is seen in a room with the other fighters doing some warm-ups. While the other fighters are doing standard warm-ups, like jumping rope or punching, Amoeba waggles his body back and forth, which weirds out Gama. 

Jed enters the training room wearing a cloak and quickly defeats all of the Floor Masters. Amoeba is seen lying on the ground with puncture wounds on his neck and an empty vial of Jed's blood beside him. 

After being trapped in a room with Amoeba and eight of the other Floor Masters, Gon and Killua get attacked by Kanjiru and get sent flying back. Gon lands against Amoeba, who grumbles at Gon. 

Later, while Gon tries to dodge Dani's attacks, Amoeba ambushes Gon from behind using his Amoeba Attack. Even after the Floor Masters in the room are knocked out, they get back up, walking in a zombified manner after Gon and Killua. The two boys manage to escape from the room using the elevator, leaving the Floor Masters behind. 

Abilities & Powers

Not much is known about Amoeba's abilities, but the fact that he was a Floor Master is proof enough of his power and proficiency in Nen. Still, he was defeated single-handedly by Jed. When fighting Gon and Killua, he is shown to fire an unknown liquid from his right sleeve at high pressure. Whether this is, a form of Nen or a hidden weapon is unknown. In the magazine held by Killua, he is seen dodging an attack by leaping backward over his opponent, demonstrating a good deal of agility. 

Magazine Finishers

Amoeba Attack (Ameeba Kougeki): Amoeba shoots a liquid from his palm. Whether it damages the opponent with its pressure or it has other effects, is unknown. 

Mikawashi (possibly "Double Exchange"): When attacked, Amoeba dodges by somersaulting over his opponent, landing behind them. "Kawashi" is sometimes used in martial arts to indicate a reaction to a blow in which one does not block or move away, but rather goes toward the attacker and trades places with them. 

Trivia

Amoeba is named after the genus of protists of the same name.
